verses.text: After a long siege the city is broken up, and the princes of Babylon sit in the middle gate. Jerusalem falls. The judgment so long foretold has come to pass.
After a long siege the city is broken up, and the princes of Babylon sit in the middle gate. Jerusalem falls. The judgment so long foretold has come to pass.
After a long siege the city is broken up, and the princes of Babylon sit in the middle gate. Jerusalem falls. The judgment so long foretold has come to pass.

Zedekiah flees but is caught; his sons are slain before his eyes, then his eyes are put out, and he is bound for Babylon. His doom is terrible. He saw the king of Babylon, as foretold, then saw no more.

They burn the palace and houses, break down the walls, and carry the people away, leaving only the poor as vinedressers. The city is desolate. The proud are exiled; the lowly remain.
They burn the palace and houses, break down the walls, and carry the people away, leaving only the poor as vinedressers. The city is desolate. The proud are exiled; the lowly remain.

Nebuchadnezzar orders that Jeremiah be well treated; he is freed and committed to Gedaliah, dwelling among the people. God cares for His servant. The prophet who suffered for the truth is honoured at the last.

God promises Ebed-melech, 'I will deliver thee... because thou hast put thy trust in me.' His kindness is repaid. The man who risked himself for God's servant is kept safe in the day of ruin.
